verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 is a canonical ER-resident chaperone (KDEL-retained, ER-luminal). Baseline: cytoplasmic/endomembrane-resident. However, it is well-documented to translocate to the outer leaflet of tumor cells and stressed cells (apoptosis, severe ER stress, UPR activation), where it is recognized as a cancer-associated ecto-antigen and is the target of cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs (CAR-T, antibody programs). The cancer-cell and cell-death ecto-pool is stable and exploitable, qualifying as contextual cell_state_induced surface display. Pre-no checklist: cell_state_induced ✓ (cancer & cell death); tissue_restricted ✗ (not lineage-restricted, appears in stress context broadly); lysosomal_exocytosis ✗ (not a lysosomal TM protein); dual_localization ✗ (no baseline PM pool documented); stable_surface_attachment ✗ (no covalent or wash-resistant TM-partner tethering).
